questions.question: A cylindrical water tank has a radius of 4 feet and a height of 10 feet. What is the volume of the tank in cubic feet? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder is V = πr²h. Substitute r = 4 and h = 10: V = 3.14 × 16 × 10 = 502.4 cubic feet.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 6 cm and a height of 8 cm. If a sphere with the same volume is created, what is the volume both shapes share? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of cone: V = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3) × 3.14 × 36 × 8 = 301.44 cm³. The sphere has the same volume by the problem statement.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 inches by 7 inches by 12 inches. A smaller rectangular prism with half the volume is created. What is the volume of the smaller prism in cubic inches?
questions.explanation: Volume of original prism: V = 5 × 7 × 12 = 420 cubic inches. Half the volume: 420 ÷ 2 = 210 cubic inches.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 3 meters. Which expression gives its volume in cubic meters? (Use π in your answer)
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. Substitute r = 3: V = (4/3)π(27) = 36π cubic meters, or approximately 113.04 m³.

questions.question: A cone and a cylinder have identical base radii of 5 inches and identical heights of 12 inches. What is the ratio of the cone's volume to the cylinder's volume? Express as a simplified fraction.
questions.explanation: Volume of cone: (1/3)πr²h. Volume of cylinder: πr²h. The ratio is [(1/3)πr²h] ÷ [πr²h] = 1/3.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 24 square cm and a height (length) of 9 cm. What is the volume of the prism?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism is V = (base area) × height. V = 24 × 9 = 216 cubic cm.
